entry.title: How can I remove the File Name in the Subreport tab?
entry.content:
Hello,

I am using SAP Crystal Reports 2016 and have a question regarding subreports.

I created a report by inserting a subreport using "Insert Subreport → Choose an existing report". After inserting it, I noticed that when I right-click the subreport and go to:

Format Subreport → Subreport tab

there is a File Name field that still contains the original file path of the subreport.

The issue is that this file was only a temporary file and has already been deleted. However, the file path information still remains in the main report.

Even though the original file no longer exists, the report runs and displays correctly without any errors.

My questions are:

Is it possible to remove or clear the File Name (file path) from the subreport properties?
Could leaving this invalid file path cause any potential issues in the future, even if the report currently works fine?

I would appreciate any guidance or best practices regarding this.

entry.title: The application 'Test your Processes' is not running.
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public cloud Edition ,When running in the app 'Test your Processes', if you run a test process created in the 'Manage Your Test Processes' app, the percentage number should gradually increase, but it stops at 0%.

How can this be resolved?

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.Create own test processes with the "Manage Your Test Processes" app. This time, I registered the procedures for the J58 scope item myself.

2.Run the created test process in the 'test your processes' app

3.It stays at '0% complete, in process' and doesn't move for a whole day.

entry.title: How to Exclude Purchase Account (EIN) from Material Ledger (ML) Closing
entry.content:
We are using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Purchase Account (transaction key EIN/EINKK) activated.

While performing the Material Ledger (ML) closing, I noticed that ML settlement postings are being generated against the Purchase Account. I only needed the pure purchase amount in this account, but I found that settlement amounts are being distributed to it as well.

Could anyone who knows how to prevent the ML closing from posting to the Purchase Account please share the solution?

Thank you.

entry.title: 切换到 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition中全新的业务伙伴主数据批量处理的应用
entry.content:
前言

你是否仍在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 中使用已被弃用的业务伙伴（Business Partner，简称 BP）主数据批量导入和批量维护应用？

现在正是时候切换到新的继任应用（Successor Apps），以便在旧应用被正式移除之前完成系统准备。本篇文章将为你介绍：

  • 为什么需要进行切换
  • 新应用带来的主要改进
  • 切换所需的具体步骤和注意事项

⚠️ 重要提醒
在旧应用被彻底下线之前，你必须完成本文描述的切换操作。

当前计划：旧应用将在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2608 版本中移除。

为什么要切换到新的批处理应用？

新的业务伙伴主数据批处理应用在多个方面得到了显著增强：

1. 更好的易用性（Usability）

  • 启动批量变更流程时，提供了全新的对话框
  • 可以更加灵活地定义和调整选择条件
  • 选择条件可在页面顶部直接查看和进一步细化
  • 不再需要单独定义变更“范围（Scope）”

➡️ 整体操作更加直观、符合业务用户的使用习惯。

 

 

2. 批量变更与批量导入更加集中

  • 现在可以在同一个起始对话框中：
    • 直接基于文件执行批量变更
    • 或进行批量数据导入
  • “管理批量维护（Manage Mass Maintenance）” 应用成为统一入口
  • 不再需要单独的 “Start Mass Processing” 磁贴

3. 更丰富的流程属性

  • 新版本支持更多流程属性，例如：
    • 到期日期（Due Date）
    • 其他流程相关信息

4. 与 SAP MDG 的更深度集成

  • 对于已购买 SAP Master Data Governance（MDG） 许可证的客户：
    • 可在 “Manage Business Partner – Central Governance” 中查看单个业务伙伴
    • 实现业务伙伴的统一视图
  • 可通过后文提到的 Additional Scope Items 启用此能力

5. 最新的 SAP Fiori 用户体验

  • 使用最新的 SAP Fiori 设计语言
  • 支持通用 Fiori 功能，例如：
    • 与其他用户协作
    • 更一致的交互体验

我需要做什么？（切换步骤概览）

完成切换总共需要 三个基础步骤：

步骤一：完成或清理已有批量流程

  • 请先：
    • 完成所有仍在进行中的批量维护流程
    • 或删除不再需要的流程

📌 原因说明
使用旧版 “Manage Mass Maintenance” 创建的流程，在新版本应用中将不可见。

步骤二：激活新功能并调整业务目录

1️⃣ 激活功能开关

激活功能：

Successor MDG apps for business partners
(MDC_BP_CLOUD_READY)

⚠️ 注意：该功能激活后不可回退，请先在测试环境验证相关业务流程。

请打开系统配置里的Activate Features来激活此功能：

 

2️⃣ 移除已弃用的业务目录

从业务角色中移除以下目录（Deprecated）：

  • Mass Load - Business Partner Master
    SAP_CMD_BC_BP_MASSLOAD_PC
  • Mass Maintenance - Business Partner Master
    SAP_CMD_BC_BP_MASSMAINT_PC

3️⃣ 分配新的继任业务目录

添加新的业务目录：

  • Master Data Governance - Mass Maintenance for Business Partners
    SAP_CMD_BC_BPCS_MAINT_PC

这将使你的业务角色与最新版模板保持一致：

  • Master Data Specialist - Business Partner Data
    SAP_BR_BUPA_MASTER_SPECIALIST

针对2️⃣3️⃣两步的操作，你可以在维护业务角色中，找到你做批量维护的业务角色，手动删除废弃的业务目录，和添加新的业务目录：

 或者

你也可以在应用“在升级后管理业务角色更改”中，打开“不赞成使用的业务目录”页签，选择被废弃的业务目录

 然后对受影响的业务角色直接做更改。这样操作会直接把被废弃的业务目录替换成后继新的业务目录：

 

步骤三：通过导出完成技术设置（关键步骤）

这是一个强制性步骤，用于最终启用所有新应用。

操作步骤：

  1. 启动应用
    Export Master Data – Business Partners
    （Fiori ID：F6551A）

  2. 定义一个最小范围的导出，例如：

    • 表：BUT000
    • 至少选择 一个业务伙伴

  3. 执行导出

    • 不需要下载生成的文件
    • 只要导出成功完成即可

✅ 完成后，即可正式使用新应用：

  • Manage Mass Maintenance – BP（Fiori ID：F7974）
  • 以及新业务目录中包含的所有继任应用

其他扩展范围（Additional Scope Items）

如果你拥有 SAP Master Data Governance 的额外许可证，还可以启用以下 Scope Item：

  • Central Governance for Business Partner (1N7)

这将为业务伙伴主数据治理提供更多高级能力。

总结

通过切换到新的业务伙伴主数据批处理应用，你将获得：

  • 更现代化的 SAP Fiori 体验
  • 更灵活、更直观的批量处理方式
  • 与 SAP MDG 更深度的集成
  • 为未来版本升级（2608 及以后）提前做好准备

👉 建议尽早完成切换，并在生产系统启用前充分测试。

原文出处

SAP Community Blog：
Switch to the new mass apps for business partner master data on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: SAP Make-to-Order Policy: When entering the manufacturing version and storage location, the manufact
entry.content:
We have set up a make-to-order strategy (strategy 20) in the material master.

We specify a storage location for the sales order line item.

We also specify the same storage location for the manufacturing version's goods receipt storage.

When we run MRP in this state, no manufacturing versions are selected at all.

The requirement is that the storage locations for component items should be automatically suggested based on the requirements of individual segments.

We cannot use the storage location for the sales order BOM line item because it is not permitted by customer requirements.

We also understand that the MRP area cannot be used in a make-to-order scenario.

Do you have any good solutions?

entry.title: Re: Tyre Tracking Requirement – Design Confirmation & Per...
entry.content:
Your current design (serialized equipment) is valid and commonly used, but performance validation is essential. Yes, you should raise this concern with DAP early, so they can advise on scalability, indexing, and data management strategies in the public cloud.

If the client is open to alternatives, propose the tyre master + lifecycle transaction model as a lighter option, or a hybrid approach depending on compliance needs.

Instead of treating each tyre as serialized equipment, maintain a tyre master record and capture lifecycle events (install, dismantle, retread, swap) as transactional logs. This reduces the overhead of serialized objects while still preserving history. Use serialization only for tyres that require strict compliance tracking (e.g., high-value or safety-critical tyres), while others are tracked at batch level. Implement periodic archiving of old tyre records to keep active datasets lean.

entry.title: Re: Vendor Master Creation Date Not Available in BP After...
entry.content: In SAP S/4HANA, the vendor creation date is no longer directly visible in transaction BP. Instead, you need to retrieve it from database tables (e.g., BUT000 for Business Partner creation date, LFA1 for vendor master data) or via change documents (CDHDR/CDPOS). The original ECC vendor creation date is not automatically migrated into BP display fields

entry.title: Re: Handling of Round-Off Values in SAP CAR and Integrati...
entry.content: Round-off values should be treated as explicit line items in CAR to ensure invoice-level balancing. During integration, map these to a dedicated rounding difference G/L account in S/4HANA. This approach is SAP’s recommended best practice, ensuring consistency across CAR and S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: WBS element search and filtering in enterprise projects
entry.content: Apart from the Project Control app, WBS elements can be searched using transactions like CJ13 or CN41N, or via reports such as the Project Information System. In S/4HANA, CDS views or custom Fiori apps can provide flexible search and filtering. For easier navigation, it’s best practice to use consistent coding masks and leverage CN41N for cross-project searches.

entry.title: Managing Content Change Notification Issues During SAP Fiori Launchpad Content Exposure to SAP BTP
entry.content:
With the SAP Fiori launchpad content exposure, you can integrate launchpad content from a product into SAP BTP by using an automatic synchronization. During the automatic synchronization, it may happen that the exposure log receives the following error message “Notification for content channel ‘xyz’ not sent. Manual fetch required” (Message Class: /UI2/CDM3_EXP, Message Number: 399).

As you may not immediately recognize that this issue happened, this blog post provides a way of how you can monitor this type of error message using email notification from SAP Cloud Application Lifecycle Management (SAP Cloud ALM).

In short, in the SAP Cloud ALM Integration & Exception Monitoring app, you do the following:

  1. Enable the ABAP application log for the system          
  2. Add a filter for the error message
  3. Add a notification for the error

As an example, we use an SAP S/4HANA system.

Disclaimer: This article is written from the viewpoint of a consumer of SAP Cloud ALM. If you have SAP Cloud ALM-specific questions, such as how to set up your system in SAP Cloud ALM, please reach out to SAP Cloud ALM directly.

Check the following SAP Help Portal pages to see whether you are affected by this issue:

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

  • Enable Content Change Notifications
  • Display Launchpad Content Exposure Logs

SAP Integrated Business Planning

  • Enable Content Change Notifications
  • Display Launchpad Content Exposure Logs

SAP S/4HANA on-premise

  • Manage Content Change Notifications
  • Launchpad Content Exposure Troubleshooting - Error Analysis   

Prerequisites

Before we can start with the parts specific to launchpad exposure in the SAP Cloud ALM configuration, you set up your system in SAP Cloud ALM.

In addition, please ensure that you have the Integration Architect role. See SAP Cloud ALM overview page.

Enabling ABAP Application Log for Your System

First, you configure your system to collect data for the ABAP Application Log.

  1. In SAP Cloud ALM, in the Operations space, open the Integration & Exception Monitoring app.
  2. Now, let’s add a system tile to the overview: In the upper right corner, choose the Select a Scope icon and filter for your system. Select it in the table and choose Apply.
  3. On the overview page, choose the system tile and from its menu, choose Edit Configuration.
  4. For your system, toggle the Data Collection to On.
  5. For your system, choose the arrow icon to go to the details page.
  6. Activate the ABAP Application Log entry.

Adding a Filter for the Launchpad Content Exposure Error Message

Now, let’s create a filter so that we can monitor when the error message “Notification for content channel ‘xyz’ not sent. Manual fetch required” is written to the ABAP Application Log.

  1. In the same screen as above, for the ABAP Application Log, enter the monitoring and events view.
  2. To add the filter, in the Filters table, choose Add.
  3. Enter a filter name. Then, enter the following filter parameters:

    Parameter

    Operator

    Value

    Object

    Is

    /UI2/BE

    Subobject

    Is

    /UI2/CDM3

    Message ID

    Is

    /UI2/CDM3_EXP

    Message Number

    Is

    399

    Status

    Is

    E


  4. Save the filter by choosing the Save button at the bottom of the dialog.

Adding an Event to Be Notified About an Error

The last step is to create an event so that you get notified once the filter which we have created in the previous step detects the launchpad content exposure error message.

Please note that this event will not only be initiated for the launchpad content exposure error message. If you had configured other ABAP Application Log filters in addition, the event will be initiated as well. The reason is that on the date of publication of this blog post, according to 3452860 - How to setup filters for the category "ABAP application log" in Exception Monitoring of SAP Cloud ALM, it is not possible to add filters to the event itself.

  1. To create the event, navigate to the Events tab and choose Add.
  2. As Event Name, choose Erroneous ABAP Application Log and enter a display name.
  3. Define the actions: Activate Create Alert. For example, you can also configure the event to send an email once there is an error written into the ABAP Application Log.
     
  4. Save your entries.

Checking the Event

In the Integration & Exception Monitoring app, you can view all events that you have created and that were initiated on the Alerting tab.

  1. Choose the Alerting tab.
  2. Once the launchpad content exposure error happens, you can see it in the table

Conclusion:

This blog post covered how to proactively monitor HTTP 401 content change notification failures during SAP Fiori launchpad content exposure to SAP BTP using SAP Cloud ALM's Integration & Exception Monitoring app. You have learned how to enable ABAP Application Log data collection, create a targeted filter for message /UI2/CDM3_EXP 399, and configure automated email notifications to detect synchronization issues before they impact your users. By implementing this monitoring approach, you can transform reactive troubleshooting into proactive incident management, ensuring continuous content availability between your on-premise systems and SAP BTP.

If you have questions about the configuration steps or would like to share your experiences with launchpad content exposure monitoring, please feel free to leave a comment below.

entry.title: Re: Document Splitting Error in case of BS to BS posting...
entry.content:
Hi Hitesh,

A single Profit Center works because SAP does not trigger document splitting in that case; it simply inherits the Profit Center to all relevant lines. As stated in SAP documentation: “It (system) can only inherit the account assignments if the account assignment combination within the same document is unique.”

When multiple Profit Centers are used, this uniqueness no longer exists. SAP then switches to document splitting and attempts to derive the Profit Center using the configured basis item categories. In your simulation screenshot, both the deriving line and the line carrying the Profit Center are balance sheet items, so SAP is unable to determine a unique basis for derivation.

For manual postings, this can be resolved by either posting the entries separately per Profit Center or manually maintaining the Profit Center on all relevant line items.

Regards,

Anand Raichura

entry.title: Re: KE28: Additional "Process" section is display...
entry.content:
The additional “Process” section in transaction KE28 (with fields like Post Receivers with Amounts From and Currency Type for Amount Check) is an expected standard change introduced in SAP S/4HANA 2025. It is part of SAP’s ongoing enhancements to allocation and balancing functionality, not an unintended issue. Functionally, it provides more control over invoice-level checks and postings, but does not alter the core KE28 processing logic.

The “Process” section is documented in SAP’s upgrade guides and simplification items as a new configuration area for handling allocation checks and currency consistency. Check this SAP Help document Upgrade Guide for SAP S/4HANA 2025 

In earlier releases (e.g., 2023), these options were either implicit or handled via customizing. Now they are exposed directly in the KE28 screen.

entry.title: Re: Deleting records from replicated table
entry.content:
Hi @silverado23,

In a replication flow, the delete or truncate option takes effect only when the replication flow is restarted.

For example, if you create a new replication flow and ingest data into a local table, the initial load will ingest and write records to the local table regardless of the refresh behavior configured (delete or truncate). These refresh options are applied only after the replication flow is restarted.

Given your problem statement, one possible approach would be to build a parallel SQL view that handles the required selective deletes.

entry.title: Re: Story Reports: Data Export in View Mode
entry.content:
Dear Varun,

thank you for your response and for clarifying the intended principle behind Story Reports.

I would like to respectfully challenge the assumption that, in practice, users always see the same data in Reporting that they can see in the application itself. While this may be the general principle, there are notable and intentional exceptions.

Besides Recruiting, Learning is another prominent example where reporting access is deliberately broader than application access. In Learning, we provide users with aggregated reporting insights (e.g. completion rates, participation statistics, averages) while explicitly not granting them access to the underlying learner level data in the application UI. These reports were intentionally designed to balance transparency with data protection and governance requirements.

From a data protection and compliance perspective, the ability to export data in mass significantly changes the risk profile compared to on screen visibility alone.

In particular:

  • Mass download enables data to be stored, forwarded, or combined outside the system, beyond technical access controls and auditability.
  • This directly conflicts with core data protection principles such as data minimization, purpose limitation, and least privilege access.
  • Even if users are authorized to view certain aggregated information, this does not automatically justify the ability to extract row level personal data, especially in a reusable and persistent format.

In many organizations, strict internal policies and works council agreements differentiate clearly between:

  • viewing aggregated insights for operational or management purposes, and
  • extracting detailed personal data, which is usually restricted to a much smaller and explicitly authorized audience.

If the export capability in view mode remains unchanged, we will have no practical alternative but to revoke access to a large number of existing Recruiting and Learning Story Reports. These reports were created precisely on the premise that users could benefit from aggregated information without gaining access to the underlying data or the application objects themselves. Removing them from self service would represent a significant regression for end users and increase manual reporting effort for administrators.

For these reasons, while we acknowledge the value of the feature for selected use cases, we strongly believe that some form of control (permission based or report level configuration) is essential to allow customers to safely adopt it without compromising data protection, compliance, or existing reporting concepts.

Kind regards
Sebastian

entry.title: Re: User status A is not getting updated when notificatio...
entry.content:
If I’m understanding your requirement correctly, you want the notification created through a work order to automatically receive User Status A. In that case, the first thing to consider is whether User Status A should simply be configured as the initial status in OIBS. That would be the cleanest solution if every notification of that type should start with that status. However, if the same notification type can also be created manually or through other channels—where User Status A should only be applied once it put in process then a more controlled approach is needed.

There are two practical ways to handle this. The most direct and reliable option is User Exit IWO10009. This enhancement is triggered when the notification is saved, allowing you to add logic that checks whether the notification was created from a work order (for example, by validating the order reference field). If the condition is met, the system can programmatically assign User Status A using STATUS_CHANGE_EXTERN. This approach gives you precise control without relying on broader transaction settings.

The second option is to ensure that you have selected all put in process or notification in process option in OIBS. In addition , you can also configure a work order assign business transaction to update the linked notification with User Status A. This can work well if the status should align with a defined order lifecycle step. However, it requires careful validation because it may also set the status in scenarios where it is not intended, depending on how the order process is used.

In short, if the requirement is conditional and tied specifically to notifications originating from work orders, the user exit approach is typically the safer and more flexible solution. If the business rule is process-driven and consistent across the order lifecycle, OIBS transaction control can be considered.

entry.title: Usage of Catalog Items outside of Purchase Requisitions
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a question regarding the following KBA Note which was published yesterday:

3745044 -  Using the Cross Catalog Search , the system throws a runtime dump. - SAP for Me

This note contains the following statement.

 

Cause

Catalog item price field supports up to 23 digits with 4 decimal places

PR valuation price field supports only up to 11 digits with 2 decimal places

if a catalog item is intended to be added to a Purchase Requisition via Cross Catalog Search, the item's price must be created within the value range that the PR valuation price field can accommodate — i.e., a maximum of 11 digits total with up to 2 decimal places. Creating catalog items with prices that exceed this range will result in a system dump when those items are added to a PR.

Resolution

Ensure catalog item prices do not exceed the DEC(11,2) limit when those items are expected to flow into Purchase Requisitions.

 

👉  That raises the question for me: 

➡️  In what situations are catalog items created that are not intended to be included in purchase requisitions? In our company, catalog items always flow into purchase requisitions, otherwise we wouldn't create them.

➡️  Are there companies where catalog items are handled differently? Do you procure catalog items directly without first creating Purchase Requisitions?

 

I would appreciate input / examples of other practical approaches 🙂

 

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: Re: PR/PO details in IW38/IW39 report
entry.content:
Using IW38/IW39, you will not be able to directly view the associated PR/PO for a work order. The “Purchase Order” field in these transactions is typically a manually entered customer PO reference captured on the notification, mainly for external/customer tracking purposes—it is not the system-generated purchasing document.

Actual PRs and POs related to work orders are generally created through external operations, non-stock materials, or direct procurement items. Because of that, procurement visibility exists at the item level rather than the order header level.

To review those details, the better standard options are:

  • IW3L for component-level visibility
  • IW49N for operation-level visibility
  • ME2K to list all POs tied to the work order header via account assignment

A particularly effective standard approach that I mostly use is IW40 (Multilevel Order Display). By enabling both Operations and Components in the field selection, you can drill into the detailed structure of the order. In the display settings, choose the PR field under operations and components to surface purchasing references directly within the multilevel view. Limit all other information to keep clean output display.

hope this is helpful!

entry.title: Re: Taxability Model - US
entry.content: The Tax Area is  not meant to identify the area or location ( zip codes) where  the employee resides or works . A number of  areas or locations or a number of zip codes can have the same Tax Area.  Tax area is rather a combination of applicable tax authorities with their individual jurisdiction.

entry.title: Use of Service Materials (DIEN) in Advanced Intercompany Sales (5D2)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am currently working on a project where we need to map a service sales flow to a customer. This service is executed by a subsidiary company within the same group.

Regarding Scope Item 5D2 (Advanced Intercompany Sales), I have the following questions:

  1. Can I use a DIEN (Service) material type directly in this flow?

  2. If DIEN is not supported, would it be a valid workaround to create a custom material type (e.g., copied from FERT) but configured as non-valued and non-stockable?

I want to ensure the advanced intercompany logic (including the automated intercompany billing and postings) triggers correctly for a service-based scenario.

Has anyone implemented a similar flow for services using 5D2, or is there a better scope item for intercompany services?

Thanks in advance for your insights!

entry.title: Create Custom Search View and Custom Result View SAP CRM Web UI
entry.content:
Step 1: Create a Database table in se11 to hold the values to be searched and displayed.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Entries in the Custom Database Table:

 

 

 

Step 2: Create a Structure in se11

Step 3: Create Two Tables (Object Table to hold the BOL Objects and Model Table to hold the relationship between the Objects):

Step 4:  Create a New class in se24 with Super Class as CL_CRM_GENIL_ABSTR_COMPONENT2.

Redefine the below Methods and add coding the below methods:

  • IF_GENIL_APPL_INTLAY~GET_DYNAMIC_QUERY_RESULT
  • IF_GENIL_APPL_INTLAY~GET_OBJECTS
  • IF_GENIL_APPL_MODEL~GET_MODEL
  • IF_GENIL_APPL_MODEL~GET_OBJECT_PROPS

 

 

 

 

 

Redefine the Below Methods:

 

 

 

 

method IF_GENIL_APPL_MODEL~GET_MODEL.
***********************************************************************
*In this Method we select data from the Model table
***********************************************************************
SELECT * FROM ztab_model1
INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS
OF TABLE rt_relation_det.

endmethod.

 

 

 

 

method IF_GENIL_APPL_MODEL~GET_OBJECT_PROPS.
*****************************************************************************
* In this method we select the data from the Object Table
*****************************************************************************
SELECT * FROM ztab_object1
INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE rt_obj_props.
endmethod.

 

 

 

 

 

METHOD if_genil_appl_intlay~get_objects.
****************************************************************************
* In this method we populate the BOL structures and read object attributes 
****************************************************************************
  DATA: lr_object           TYPE REF TO   if_genil_container_object,
        lr_msg_cont         TYPE REF TO   cl_crm_genil_global_mess_cont,
        lv_name_obj         TYPE          ztab_det1-product_id,
        lr_attr_props       TYPE REF TO   if_genil_obj_attr_properties,
        l_result            TYPE          zst_det1,
        lv_name             TYPE          crmt_ext_obj_name.

  lr_object = iv_root_list->get_first( ).
  lr_msg_cont ?= iv_root_list->get_global_message_container( ).
  WHILE lr_object IS BOUND.
    lv_name = lr_object->get_name( ).
    IF lr_object->check_attr_requested( ) = abap_true.
      CALL METHOD lr_object->get_key
        IMPORTING
          es_key = lv_name_obj.
      SELECT SINGLE * FROM ztab_det1
        INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF
        l_result WHERE product_id = lv_name_obj.
      lr_object->set_attributes( l_result ).
      lr_attr_props = lr_object->get_attr_props_obj( ).
      lr_attr_props->set_all_properties( if_genil_obj_attr_properties=>read_only ).
    ENDIF.      "End of IF lr_object->check_attr_requested( ) = abap_true.
    lr_object = iv_root_list->get_next( ).
  ENDWHILE.     "End of WHILE lr_object IS BOUND.
ENDMETHOD.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

METHOD if_genil_appl_intlay~get_dynamic_query_result.
  TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_gen,
                     sign TYPE bapisign,
                     option TYPE bapioption,
                     low TYPE string ,
                     high TYPE string ,
         END OF ty_gen.
  TYPES: tt_gen TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_gen .
  DATA:  lr_object       TYPE REF TO if_genil_cont_root_object,
         lt_result       TYPE TABLE OF ztab_det1,
         l_result        TYPE zst_det1,
         lv_dyn_where    TYPE string,
         lv_len          TYPE i ,
         ls_range        TYPE ty_gen,
         lt_product_id   TYPE tt_gen,
         lt_load_truck   TYPE tt_gen,
         lt_truck_num    TYPE tt_gen,
         lt_del_des      TYPE tt_gen.
  FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lfs_result> TYPE zst_det1,
  <lfs_selection_range> TYPE genilt_selection_parameter.
* In this section we form the query and get the data from the Database table
  IF it_selection_parameters[] IS NOT INITIAL.
    LOOP AT it_selection_parameters[] ASSIGNING <lfs_selection_range>.
      MOVE: <lfs_selection_range>-sign TO ls_range-sign ,
      <lfs_selection_range>-option TO ls_range-option ,
      <lfs_selection_range>-low TO ls_range-low ,
      <lfs_selection_range>-high TO ls_range-high.
      CASE <lfs_selection_range>-attr_name.
        WHEN 'PRODUCT_ID'.
          APPEND ls_range TO lt_product_id[].
          CONCATENATE lv_dyn_where 'product_id in lt_product_id[] and' INTO lv_dyn_where SEPARATED BY space .
        WHEN 'LOAD_TRUCK'.
          APPEND ls_range TO lt_load_truck[].
          CONCATENATE lv_dyn_where 'load_truck in lt_load_truck[] and' INTO lv_dyn_where SEPARATED BY space .
        WHEN 'TRUCK_NUM'.
          APPEND ls_range TO lt_truck_num[].
          CONCATENATE lv_dyn_where 'truck_num in lt_truck_num[] and' INTO lv_dyn_where SEPARATED BY space .
        WHEN 'DEL_DES'.
          APPEND ls_range TO lt_truck_num[].
          CONCATENATE lv_dyn_where 'del_des in lt_del_des[] and' INTO lv_dyn_where SEPARATED BY space .
      ENDCASE.
    ENDLOOP."End of LOOP AT it_selection_parameters[] ASSIGNING      <lfs_selection_range>.
    IF lv_dyn_where IS NOT INITIAL.
      TRANSLATE lv_dyn_where TO UPPER CASE.
      lv_len = strlen( lv_dyn_where ) - 4 . "remove the last 'AND' in the condition
      lv_dyn_where = lv_dyn_where(lv_len) . "assign the query
      CHECK strlen( lv_dyn_where ) > 0.
      SELECT * FROM ztab_det1 INTO TABLE lt_result[] WHERE (lv_dyn_where).
    ELSE.
      SELECT * FROM ztab_det1 INTO TABLE lt_result.
    ENDIF.    "End of IF lv_dyn_where IS NOT INITIAL.
    CHECK lines( lt_result[] ) > 0.
  ELSEIF it_selection_parameters[] IS  INITIAL.
    SELECT * FROM ztab_det1 INTO TABLE lt_result.
  ENDIF.  "End of IF it_selection_parameters[] IS NOT INITIAL.
  LOOP AT lt_result INTO l_result.
    lr_object = iv_root_list->add_object( iv_object_name = 'TestRoot'
                              is_object_key  = l_result-product_id ).
    lr_object->set_query_root( abap_true ).
  ENDLOOP. "End of LOOP AT lt_result INTO l_result.
ENDMETHOD.

Step 5: Make the Entry in spro under CRM->CRM Cross Application Components -> Generic Interaction Layer / Object Layer -> Basic Settings

Step 6: Now we shall create Search View and Result View

Step 7:  Add the BOL Object created to the Model

Step 8: Create the Custom Controller:

 

 

 

 

 

Step 9:  Create the Search View with Two Context Nodes (One for doing Search and other for holding Search Result). Bind the Result Context Node in Search View with the Custom Controller.

Choose the View Type as Empty View:

Now change Super Class of Implementation Class to CL_BSP_WD_ADVSEARCH_CONTROLLER

Change Super Class to CL_BSP_WD_ADVSEARCH_CONTROLLER.

Click on YES and Activate the Class.

Now Change Super Class of Search Context Node to CL_BSP_WD_CONTEXT_NODE_ASP

Go to Edit Mode and Click on Change Inheritence.

Activate the class.

Now add the coding in.htm page of the Search view as below:

<%@page language="abap" %>
<%@extension name="htmlb" prefix="htmlb" %>
<%@extension name="xhtmlb" prefix="xhtmlb" %>
<%@extension name="crm_bsp_ic" prefix="crmic" %>
<%@extension name="bsp" prefix="bsp" %>
<%@extension name="chtmlb" prefix="chtmlb" %>
<%@extension name="thtmlb" prefix="thtmlb" %>
<%
  DATA:
  lv_xml    TYPE string.
  lv_xml = controller->configuration_descr->get_config_data( ).
%>
<chtmlb:overviewFormConfig xml  = "<%= lv_xml %>"
               mode = "RUNTIME" />

<thtmlb:advancedSearch id                = "advs0"
fieldMetadata     = "<%= controller->GET_DQUERY_DEFINITIONS( ) %>"
header            = "<%= TestSearch->get_param_struct_name( ) %>"
fieldNames        = "<%= controller->GET_POSSIBLE_FIELDS( ) %>"
values            = "//TestSearch/PARAMETERS"
maxHits           = "//TestSearch/MAX_HITS"
onEnter           = "SEARCH"
 ajaxDeltaHandling = "false" />

 <thtmlb:button  id      = "SEARCH_BTN"
                 text    = "SEARCH"
                 onClick = "search1"
                         />

 Now do the configuration for the Search Page:

Step 10: Result View: Now let us create the Result View:

Go to the Configuration Tab and add the fields from available fields to displayed fields.

Step 11: Now create a Viewset having two View Areas and add Search view to View area1 and Result View to View area 2.

Now assign ViewSet to the Window.

Step 12:  Create an Event handler in Search view and add the code to do the Search using the Search BOL Object and put to the Result Context Node of the Search View.

By this when the User clicks on Search button the Event handler will get triggerred and it does the Search using the Search BOL Object and puts to result context node of Search view. This data is passed to Custom Controller and passed to Context Node of Result View.

 

 

 

 

 

method EH_ONSEARCH1.
****************************************************************************
*When the User clicks on Search button the Event handler will get triggerred *and it does the Search using the Search BOL Object and puts to result *context node of Search view.
*This data is passed to Custom Controller and passed to Context Node of *Result View.
****************************************************************************
DATA: query_service TYPE REF TO cl_crm_bol_dquery_service.
DATA: result TYPE REF TO if_bol_bo_col.

query_service ?= me->typed_context->TestSearch->collection_wrapper->get_current( ).
result = query_service->get_query_result( ).
me->typed_context->testresult->collection_wrapper->clear( ).
me->typed_context->testresult->set_collection( result ).
endmethod.

Now let us change the Description of the Viewset:

 

 

Now Execute the Component ZSRCMP_DET. We can see the Output as below:

 

 

 

On clicking Search Button we can see the Output in the Result View:

 

 

 

By following the above steps we can create Custom Search View and Result View.

                                                                      * End of Document *

entry.title: Re: System is not allowing us to post the Unplanned Depre...
entry.content:
Hi Rajesh,

 
Based on the error message, the issue is likely that the unplanned depreciation would reduce the asset’s NBV below the defined scrap/cutoff value in depreciation area 01. When Current NBV – unplanned depreciation < scrap/cutoff value, the system raises message AA632 and blocks the posting as per standard SAP validation.
 
Kindly check the value and advice user on the same.
 
Regards,
Anand Raichura

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Build AI services using SAP CAP and Generative AI Hub (Gurugram, India)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core and Generative AI Hub. It is beneficial if you have some basic knowledge about JavaScript as we are using the SAP Cloud SDK for AI with the JavaScript variant.

Please note that this is an advanced SAP CAP CodeJam and it requires participants to have basic understanding of the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model. 

This CodeJam introduces attendees to aspects of exposing generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) capabilities to execute tasks using a given business context.

During this event, you will become acquainted with the theory behind GenAI, SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, and learn how to use the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model to expose AI capabilities through your own OData service.

While working through the exercises, you will become comfortable creating CDS-based service definitions and integrating them into the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and SAP AI Core Foundation Models.

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have.
  • We will be using the primary environment as described in the prerequisites here https://github.com/SAP-samples/codejam-cap-llm/blob/main/prerequisites.md#prerequisites

Location and Date

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Thursday, May 21st, 2026 from 09:30 - 16:00 local time in Gurugram, India. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Event Address: Nagarro Software Private Limited, Plot 6B, Sub. Major Laxmi Chand Rd, Maruti Udyog, Sector 18, Sarhol, 122015 Gurugram, Haryana, India

Google maps location: https://maps.app.goo.gl/wwyLK7WG3Jk2Y9qw7

  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @ajay_soreng and @shrinivasan_neelamegam 
  • Thank you Nagarro Software Private Limited for hosting this event.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 35 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About two weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance via email.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: Item 2 Rendering of document failed. Messages have be...
entry.content:
Hello newguy_cloud ,

That error isn’t coming from CRM. It’s related to the key parameters defined in your form template. There is a general KBA 2772626 Error "Key parameter XXXX has not been provided" when sending Business Document Output - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. It was created for a different component, but it might still be helpful in your case.

Best regards,
Silvia

entry.title: Cloud ALM Comments are not visible
entry.content:
in Cloud ALM Implementation: When there are many comments added  for example for user story those are only displayed with "Show all" command in the side panel.

In that side panel, possible screen shots and pasted tables are not shown totally: there seems to be a limited width that is shown so part of information is cut out. No scrolling possibility.

The possible width limitation should be same when pasting data to a comment as now we are in a situation that comment seems to be saved correctly but later we cannot see complete data added to comments.

entry.title: Establish Connection between SAP SF and 3rd Party non-SF based SFTP Host for Integration Center
entry.content:
1. Introduction

This documentation outlines the specialized configuration required to establish a secure file-based integration between SAP SuccessFactors and an external 3rd-party SFTP host using the Integration Center. Unlike standard internal file transfers, this process utilizes a client-hosted server, necessitating mutual firewall allowlisting, SSH key authentication, and PGP encryption. The following sections detail the prerequisite technical requirements, the essential data exchange between both parties, and the mandatory SAP-Support actions required to enable outbound connectivity through the SAP data center firewall.

2. Problem Statement

SAP SuccessFactors (SF) typically supports file transfers using its internal SFTP server. However, in this case, the integration requires connecting to a 3rd party’s external SFTP host. This introduces challenges such as:

  • Establishing secure connectivity between SF and the external host.
  • Managing mutual whitelisting of IP addresses.
  • Handling encryption requirements with PGP keys.
  • Coordinating responsibilities across three stakeholders: 3rd Party System, SuccessFactors Team, and SAP Support.

This document outlines the integration process, divided into sub-processes, with commentary to highlight key considerations.

3. Process to establish connection

3.1 Overview

 

3.2 Requirement Gathering

Activities:

  • Collect SFTP host details (address, port, file path, user credentials).
  • Obtain host IP addresses.
  • Confirm user permissions for file path access.
  • Receive PGP encryption key from the 3rd party.

Commentary:
This step ensures all technical prerequisites are captured before configuration begins. Missing or incorrect details at this stage can delay integration or cause connectivity failures.

 

3.3 Authentication Setup

Activities:

  • Generate SSH key pair in SuccessFactors.
  • Share the public key with the 3rd party for authentication.
  • Provide the SAP SF datacentre IP to the 3rd party for whitelisting.

Steps:

 

Commentary:
Authentication is critical for secure file transfer. Using SSH keys avoids password-based vulnerabilities, while whitelisting ensures only trusted IPs can connect.

  • Category: Authentication Key (SSH)
  • Key Size: As per requirements of 3rd Party
  • Valid until: NA (For no expiry)
  • For SAP SF IP Address: Link

 

3.4 SAP Support Coordination

Activities:

  • Raise a ticket with SAP Support.
  • Request whitelisting of the 3rd party’s SFTP host IP and URL.
  • Obtain confirmation once whitelisting is complete.

Steps:

 

 

 

Commentary:
This step requires coordination with SAP Support. Without whitelisting, SuccessFactors cannot establish outbound connections to the external SFTP host.

3.5 Integration Center Configuration

Activities:

  • Configure outbound file transfer job in Integration Center.
  • Apply PGP encryption using the provided key.

Steps:

 

 

Commentary:
Integration Center acts as the execution engine for file transfers. Proper configuration ensures files are securely encrypted and delivered to the correct directory.

  • SFTP Detail (Server Host Address, Username, File Details): To be provided by 3rd party
  • Authentication Key: Created at Security Center
  • File Encryption: Imported at Security Center

3.6 Validation & Testing

Activities:

  • Perform test transfers to the external SFTP host.
  • Validate encryption, permissions, and file delivery.
  • Confirm successful end-to-end integration.

Commentary:
Testing verifies that all components—network, authentication, encryption, and file transfer—work together seamlessly. This step reduces risk before moving to production.

4. Roles and Responsibilities

Stakeholder

Responsibilities

3rd Party

Provide SFTP details, IP addresses, user permissions, PGP key and add SSH Key

SF Admin

Configure Integration Center, generate SSH key, apply encryption and testing

SAP Support

Whitelist 3rd party SFTP host IP & URL

5. Conclusion

This blog addresses the issue of using non-sf sFTP host for integration via integration center. Using non-sf sFTP bring its own set of challenges, as the firewall and security standards could differ from SAP SF based sFTP. Also, the communication and co-ordination between SAP and 3rd Party, could be challenging sometimes. But this provides the integration process an additional capability to accommodate set of rigid 3rd parties.

entry.title: CLM Lease-out revenue postings on cost center / wbs-element in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear,

We are implementing CLM in the Public Cloud and the customer wants to create and post Lease-out contracts. After activating the necessary scope items we can use the standard configuration to create Lease-out contracts, to perform the Periodic posting and to create the Invoice.

If we check the generated revenue postings in detail, we notice that these are posted on the cost center or wbs-element assigned in the contract object. These are the only two options to assign a controlling object to the Lease-out contract. Since we are posting revenues, we do not want to post on a cost center or wbs-element. In the standard settings it becomes a negative cost posting on a GL account which is set as cost element (category 1).

In the previous RE-FX solution, the Lease-out contract itself was the controlling object which generates revenues. By using object transfers the postings are done on the master data objects assigned to the Lease-out contract. In Public cloud we do not have this option anymore.

How can we achieve a correct revenue posting on a profit center / profitability segment without the cost center or wbs-element?

Thanks in advance,

Jeroen Pasman

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Update configuration button in a VC product's Configu...
entry.content:
The 'update configuration' button is used when optimization of the variant configuration products is turned on to postpone VCP calls on every change.

Please refer to this help page General Settings | SAP Help Portal for more details on this button.

It says here that "The Configuration and Pricing on Demand feature is now available for SAP CPQ Quote 2.0 and Variant Configuration and Pricing integration. This feature enables you to decide whether they you want to trigger the two processes, Rules Evaluation (configuration update), and Pricing without having to wait for backend response after each click during configuration. The application parameter Enable Varient Configuration and Pricing Calls on Demand in the General Settings tab must be enabled on the SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing Provider Settings page to enable this functionality."

It is mandatory to click on this button to have in sync CPQ configuration and VCP Configuration so if you do not need this feature you can turn it off.

entry.title: Re: The activation of parallel ledgers
entry.content:
Dear @Elaine,

In Public Cloud 1GA and 5W2 cannot be activated later in an already live productive tenant if they were not selected during initial CBC scoping. These scope items define additional ledger / valuation scenarios, not ordinary business process scope. SAP states that additional ledgers must be selected during initial scoping and cannot be added or removed after the initial scope/content activation is completed

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/configuring-sap-s4hana-cloud-public-edition-with-sap-central-business-configuration/defining-scope-and-localizations

https://help.sap.com/doc/c38e987da8cf471f8888d703938f2ef1/SHIP3.0/en-US/SAPCentralBusinessConfiguration_ProjectExperience_en-US.pdf

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3530183

For this reason creating a new overseas company code in the existing tenant does not allow activation of 1GA Group Ledger IFRS or 5W2 Group Valuation now. The new company code can only use the ledger setup already active in the system

Summary:

1GA and 5W2 are initial scoping decisions in Public Cloud. They cannot be activated retrospectively in a live tenant after initial scope/content activation

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Enterprise AI Needs More Than Generative Models
entry.content:
Generative AI has changed how we interact with systems. Writing emails, summarising documents, chatting with copilots — all of that is now table stakes. 

But when I work with enterprises, I keep seeing the same pattern: The most important business decisions are not made in text.  They are made in tables. 

Employees, sales deals, payments, supply chains, logistics, finance — these are structured datasets. Rows. Columns. Relationships. And this is where many AI initiatives quietly struggle. 

Gartner puts some hard numbers behind this reality: 

  1. 57% of organisations admit their data is not AI‑ready 
  2. Less than 30% of CEOs are satisfied with the returns from current AI investments 
  3. By 2027, task‑specific AI models are expected to outperform general‑purpose LLMs by 3x for decision accuracy 

The problem isn’t AI ambition. It’s using the right kind of AI for the right kind of data. 

Why Generative AI Alone Falls Short for Enterprise Decisions?

Large language models are excellent at natural language tasks. They predict the next word incredibly well. But enterprise systems don’t need the next word. They need the next outcome. 

When applied to structured data, general LLMs often struggle with: 

  1. Numeric accuracy 
  2. Understanding relationships across multiple fields 
  3. Explainability and governance 

That’s not a failure of the technology. It’s simply a mismatch. Structured data requires relational intelligence, not linguistic fluency. 

The Case for Relational Foundation Models 

This is where SAP RPT‑1 becomes especially relevant. 

Play around with SAP RPT-1 at https://rpt.cloud.sap/

RPT1- PlaygroundSample Use CasesTest and Try

SAP RPT‑1 is not an LLM. It is a relational pre‑trained transformer, built specifically for tabular enterprise data. Instead of predicting text, it predicts business outcomes by understanding how columns and rows influence one another. 

In practice, that difference is profound.  Let me illustrate using a few real‑world use cases I’ve been working with. 

Use Case 1: Employee Attrition Risk Intelligence 

One of the most powerful scenarios is employee attrition prediction. I have built this app EARI - using SAP RPT-1 and vibe coding. Attaching the sample screenshots for reference. I will be sharing a blog on how to build it.

Here, employees are not reduced to sentiment or survey comments alone. Instead, the model looks at structured signals such as: 

  • Tenure 
  • Salary vs market benchmark 
  • Overtime hours 
  • Months since last promotion 
  • Engagement score 
  • Performance rating 
  • Training hours 
  • Manager changes 

Based on these inputs, the model produces: 

  • A quantified attrition risk score 
  • Clear risk categories (low, medium, high) 
  • Key risk drivers contributing to the score 
  • Actionable retention recommendations 

For example: 

  • Below‑market salary and excessive overtime surfaced as dominant risk drivers 
  • The system recommended compensation review, workload redistribution, and targeted learning interventions 

This moves HR conversations from gut feel to data‑backed decisions, without treating people like black‑box probabilities. 

Use Case 2: Deal Win Probability in Sales 

Another scenario focuses on sales decision‑making. 

Sales teams typically juggle: 

  1. Deal value 
  2. Time in pipeline 
  3. Competitor presence 
  4. Product fit 
  5. Decision‑maker engagement 
  6. Budget confirmation 
  7. Historical win/loss patterns 

Instead of reacting late in the cycle, a predictive model can estimate win probability early and explain why. 

In this scenario: 

  • Structured deal data was analysed 
  • A probability score was generated 
  • Clear recommendations followed, such as focusing on ROI articulation or escalating executive engagement 

The result is not automation replacing sellers, but AI augmenting judgement — exactly where enterprise AI should land. 

Use Case 3: From Dashboards to Decisions 

Across HR, sales, finance, and supply chain, there’s a common shift happening. 

Static dashboards answer: “What happened?” 

Predictive, relational models answer: “What is likely to happen next — and what should we do about it?” 

Gartner refers to this evolution as Decision Intelligence, and predicts that by 2027, AI‑driven decision systems will augment or automate 50% of enterprise decisions. 

This is not about replacing human decision‑making. It’s about improving decision quality at scale.

Trust, Governance, and Enterprise Reality 

Another important aspect often overlooked in AI discussions is trust. 

  • Enterprise AI must be: 
  • Explainable 
  • Governed 
  • Secure 
  • Compliant 

Relational models implemented within enterprise platforms make this feasible. Data stays within controlled boundaries, predictions are traceable, and outcomes can be validated — something that becomes critical when AI directly influences people, revenue, or compliance‑sensitive processes. 

Gartner consistently stresses that AI without trust is a liability, not an advantage. 

So Where Does This Leave Generative AI? 

Generative AI is not going away — and it shouldn’t. 

It excels at: 

  1. Conversational interfaces 
  2. Summarisation 
  3. Documentation 
  4. Knowledge assistance 

But for enterprise decisions, generative AI alone is not enough. 

The future is hybrid: 

  1. Generative models for interaction and experience 
  2. Relational foundation models for prediction and decisions 

When these are combined thoughtfully, AI stops being a demo and starts becoming a business capability. 

Final Thought 

The biggest shift I’m seeing isn’t about technology. 
It’s about mindset. 

  • From experimentation to outcomes 
  • From dashboards to decisions 
  • From generic AI to domain‑specific intelligence 

Enterprises that recognise this early will move faster, decide better, and waste less time chasing AI hype that never translates into value. 

And in my experience, that’s where the real transformation begins.

entry.title: Erfolgreicher zweiter Stammtisch Basel - Freiburg / Edition Freiburg
entry.content:
Der zweite Stammtisch hat gestern stattgefunden - das erste Mal in Freiburg. Und, trotz starker Konkurrenz durch das Pokalhalbfinalspiel, war er gut besucht. 

In gemütlicher Atmosphäre haben wir uns kennengelernt und uns über SAP und Entwicklung und KI und vieles mehr ausgetauscht. Es war sehr schöner Abend, den wir gerne wiederholen werden - da waren sich alle einig. 

Der einzige Wermutstropfen war am Ende, später am Abend, die Niederlage des SC Freiburgs. Nachdem man das 0:1 noch bejubelt hatte...

Und aus unserer Sicht funktioniert das mit dem SAP Stammtisch auch multinational hervorragend. 

Bastian
